title: Node File Services, Object Storage, and CDN Architecture
description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A product sends every byte through one web process, stores uploads on an ephemeral disk, and adds a CDN without deciding authority, mutation, privacy, or failure boundaries. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce Node storage-architecture decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
